title: rubus rubellus
intro: Rubus rubellus, also known as the golden bramble or yellow-flowering raspberry, is a rare and endangered plant species native to Europe. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of phenolic compounds and flavonoids. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and exhibiting antioxidant properties. The plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried extract, and is used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions.
